articles.title: Meesho share price jumps after falling for eight straight sessions. Choice sees more upside

articles.title: Polymer currency notes proposal under consideration, says RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra
articles.summary: Speaking at the post-policy press conference on Friday, Malhotra said the consideration was still at a preliminary stage and the central bank was examining its pros and cons.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Goldman Sachs invests ₹210 crore in Groww; shares rise 4%
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs purchased 1,13,43,750 shares of Groww on 4 June at ₹185.50 per share, amounting to ₹210.42 crore. Friale Fund IV LLC, meanwhile, sold the shares of the company. Block deal data was released post-stock market hours on Thursday.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO Day 3: GMP, subscription status, other key details. Should you apply as bidding ends today?
articles.summary: CMR Green Technologies IPO price band is set at ₹182 to ₹192 per share, and the IPO lot size is 78 shares. At the upper end of the price band, the company aims to raise ₹630.88 crore from the book-building issue, which is entirely an offer for sale of 3.29 crore equity shares.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Hindustan Zinc share price tumbles 6% after reports of government's 2% stake sale plan, Vedanta also down 5%
articles.summary: Hindustan Zinc fell as much as 5.7% to an intraday low of ₹569.20 on the BSE. Shares of Vedanta, the parent company of Hindustan Zinc, also came under pressure and declined nearly 5% to ₹312.35 apiece following the report.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: JP Morgan gives 'overweight' tag to this Tata Group stock. Do you own?

articles.title: Bond yields slip after RBI's FII push: Why India's bond markets could be the biggest winner of this policy?
articles.summary: The MPC policy aligned with expectations. Significant measures were announced on foreign capital flows, external borrowing, and forex management. Bond yields softened slightly. Experts caution about inflation outlook.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gujarat Gas had a good March quarter. All eyes now on gains from amalgamation
articles.summary: Gujarat Gas Ltd's Q4 results show potential gains from restructuring with integration of group businesses, expected to unlock value, and its Ebitda estimates have been significantly raised. But investors would be closely watching the impact of the amalgamation on the company's earnings trajectory.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.summary: RBI MPC Meeting 2026: RBI revises inflation forecast for FY27, with Q1 at 4.2%, Q2 at 5.1%, Q3 at 5.9%, and Q4 at 5.4%. Governor Malhotra highlights risks but finds comfort in foodgrain stocks. MPC keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25% with 'neutral' stance.

articles.title: Rajesh Exports share price falls below ₹100, hits 5% lower circuit for 2nd straight session; here's why
articles.summary: SEBI alleges Rajesh Exports misrepresented revenue over five years, raising concerns about financial statements accuracy. Share price locked in lower circuit despite company's clarification. No fine or penalty imposed by SEBI, company confident of resolving issues.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.summary: CMR Green Technologies IPO Day 3 LIVE: CMR Green Technologies IPO GMP jumped to its highest so far of ₹70 per share. This means that shares of CMR Green Technologies are trading at ₹262 in the unofficial market, signalling a 36% listing pop.

articles.title: Oil price steadies amid US-Iran ceasefire uncertainty; Brent hovers at $95. What's the near-term outlook?

articles.title: Silver rate today falls 2% as escalating Iran-Israel tensions dash peace hopes, Gold price down 1%: Key levels to watch
articles.summary: Investor sentiment was hit after Iran-backed Hezbollah rejected a new ceasefire proposal in Lebanon, while Israel indicated that it would not withdraw its troops from the country. MCX Silver price fell over 2% to ₹2,59,167 per kg, while MCX Gold rate lost around 1% to ₹1,58,213 per 10 grams.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI, govt unveil measures to attract foreign inflows in bonds, equities
articles.summary: The RBI and government delivered on a coordinated effort towards shoring up capital flows into Indian assets to ease the pressure on the rupee, experts said
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI MPC outcome: Central bank slashes FY27 growth projections to 6.6% amid Middle East crisis
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in its June policy meeting on Friday lowered its growth projections for the financial year 2026-27 (FY27) to 6.6% from 6.9% earlier amid the Middle East crisis and elevated crude oil prices along with disruptions to the Southwest monsoon.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI holds repo rate at 5.25% despite inflation risks, cuts GDP forecast
articles.summary: RBI has trimmed its real GDP growth forecast for FY27 to 6.6% from 6.9% earlier this year, while raising its retail inflation outlook to 5.1% from 4.6%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oil rises as Hezbollah rejects ceasefire, raising fresh fears over West Asia peace
articles.summary: Oil prices rose after Hezbollah rejected a US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, reviving concerns over wider regional tensions and their impact on global energy supplies.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Centre plans to allow PE firms, sovereign wealth funds to directly participate in all greenfield infra projects
articles.summary: The Centre is planning to introduce a new public-private partnership framework to allow PE firms, pension funds, sovereign wealth funds and other large financial investors to directly participate in greenfield project development across sectors like power, railways, airports.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt nears a quarter of ₹80,000-cr asset-sale target for FY27
articles.summary: The government has raised ₹6,366.93 crore through land monetisation via Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs), while stake sales in Central Bank of India and Coal India fetched another ₹7,808.49 crore.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India remains among fastest-growing economies despite global uncertainty: Minister Prasada
articles.summary: The minister highlighted India's push in trade, manufacturing, AI and semiconductors at IIFT's Global Business Research Conference.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: All nine recent FTAs set to be operationalized within 10 months, says Goyal
articles.summary: India expects the nine FTAs signed over the past three years to be operational within 9-10 months. Minister Goyal says these agreements complement India's growth strategy by expanding market access, facilitating trade an industry, enabling movement of talent and attracting investments.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Why has the US proposed fresh tariffs on India?
articles.summary: Experts have argued that India should not fall for the pressure tactics. It should keep the latest Section 301 tariffs and bilateral trade agreements separate.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Crude falls over 1% as Israel-Lebanon ceasefire revives hopes of wider peace
articles.summary: Oil prices fell more than 1% after Israel and Lebanon agreed to renew a ceasefire, easing concerns over supply disruptions and lifting hopes of wider regional peace.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Cabinet clears ₹20,000 cr schemes for jet fuel support, cleaner Delhi vehicles
articles.summary: One-time budgetary support of up to ₹10,000 crore for OMCs to provide ATF price stabilization amid high crude prices. The budgetary support will be in the form of interest-free advances to OMCs.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: How have crude oil prices behaved during the West Asia war?
articles.summary: The West Asia war pushed Brent crude from about $70 to a peak of $126 per barrel, raising fuel costs and inflation in India, which imports 90% of its oil. Prices have eased amid Iran-US peace talks but remain elevated, keeping pressure on growth, consumers and businesses.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India-US trade deal weeks away as negotiators resolve 99% of issues, says US Ambassador Sergio Gor
articles.summary: US Ambassador Sergio Gor said negotiators have resolved 99% of outstanding issues and expect an interim trade agreement within weeks, with only technical matters left to be finalized.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s services sector maintains growth momentum in May as output expands: HSBC India Services PMI
articles.summary: The HSBC India Services Purchasing Managers’ Index, compiled by S&P Global, rose to 59.8 in May from 58.8 in April. It has continued to grow since April, after registering its weakest growth in 14 months in March.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Centre’s domestic financing nearly doubles to ₹3.6 trillion in April, easing capital spending pressure
articles.summary: Strong small savings inflows and residual domestic financing provide a cushion for the government's FY27 spending, reducing its reliance on market borrowings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why India's fertility problem may be worse than what headline figures suggest
articles.summary: Driven by changing societal aspirations, India's fertility rate has plunged below replacement levels, particularly in southern states. The desire to have more children is even less.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India GDP growth likely slowed to 7.3% in Q4 FY26: Mint poll
articles.summary: Domestic demand and government spending likely kept growth resilient in the January-March quarter despite headwinds from the West Asia war.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt trims fertilizer demand estimate after IMD downgrades monsoon forecast
articles.summary: Stocks currently stand at 19.98 million tonnes, more than 52% of the revised seasonal requirement, significantly ahead of the typical 33% benchmark at this time in the season.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Industrial output grows 4.9% in April as India shifts to new IIP series with 2022-23 base
articles.summary: The update marks the first official reading under a revised base year, which adds high-tech products and energy-related services to better reflect the modern economy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s manufacturing PMI climbs to 55.0 in May as output hits three-month high
articles.summary: Driven by domestic demand resilience, Indian factory activity outperformed estimates in May as new orders and production grew at their fastest pace since February despite persistent cost pressures.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: GST collections rise 3.2% to ₹1.94 trillion in May
articles.summary: Excluding the impact of a one-time ₹10,000-crore GST payment made by a telecom operator for spectrum allocation in May 2025, gross collections recorded an adjusted growth of 9% during May
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Powell says Fed will lose credibility if president removes officials over policy differences
articles.summary: The remarks were his first since stepping down as Federal Reserve chair.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt to help 150,000 farmers access machinery, curb stubble burning
articles.summary: The government aims to support 150,000 small farmers with ₹8,550 crore for farm equipment access, promoting mechanisation and reducing stubble burning through the establishment of custom hiring centres, enhancing productivity and resource efficiency in agriculture.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rain, thunderstorm seen in many parts, conditions favour monsoon progress: IMD
articles.summary: The IMD has forecast heavy rain in parts of northeast and south India, along with severe thunderstorms, hailstorms and winds up to 90 kmph across several regions. As heatwave conditions ease, southwest monsoon is expected to advance further over Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Lakshadweep and adjoining seas.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Is 2026 heading for its driest monsoon since 2015?
articles.summary: IMD has lowered its monsoon outlook to 90% of normal, raising concerns over heatwaves, farm output, and food prices as El Niño risks loom large.
articles.label: BEARISH
